qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Consolidate 5 independent /allow grant-read implementations into lib/allowlist.py

out_of_scope_observations.observation: IS_SUBAGENT firewall test for git-guard only verifies _check_git_allowlist returns False; does not monkeypatch read_grant_for_git_command/consume_grant_for_posttool to assert they are not called. Bash and posttool firewall paths are not directly tested for 'no lib call/no delete'. Implementation line order is correct, so this is not a functional blocker.

Path traversal via sid: no sanitization on session_id before f-string interpolation into /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-{sid}.json. A sid containing '/' could produce a path outside /tmp. However, Claude session IDs come from Claude's own runtime (UUIDs), not user-supplied input. No real exploit vector exists in the current threat model. Out of scope per cycle requirements.

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: 5 independent open/flock/JSON-load/match implementations in 4 callers have been replaced with calls to lib/allowlist.py canonical functions. posttool-allowlist-consume.py, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, pretool-bash-safety.sh (heredoc shim), and pretool-write-guard.sh all delegate to the library. All IS_SUBAGENT firewalls remain in callers. os.unlink confined to consume_grant_for_posttool. Force-push ordering invariant preserved.
qa.overall_assessment: The implementation achieves the user requirement: all 5 independent grant-read implementations are consolidated into lib/allowlist.py with invariants preserved. However, 2 ACs have literal non-compliance issues: AC1 uses 'candidates: list' annotation instead of 'candidates: list[str]', and AC8's strict 'NO reference to IS_SUBAGENT' criterion is violated by 3 comment-level mentions in allowlist.py (lines 9, 152, 177). Both are cosmetic/annotation defects with zero runtime impact; user requirement is functionally satisfied. Verdict is FAIL on literal AC grounds.

success_criteria_results.criterion: signatures match spec exactly; candidates: list[str]; read_grant signature unchanged; match_grant_for_bash_command calls _match_loaded_grant directly
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read hooks/lib/allowlist.py lines 38-50, 97-114; grep for 'candidates: list'
success_criteria_results.details: hooks/lib/allowlist.py lines 40 and 99 use 'candidates: list' annotation. AC1 specifies the parameter must be typed 'candidates: list[str]'. All other signature elements are correct: _match_loaded_grant present at line 38, _load_and_match at line 97, read_grant_for_git_command at line 146, match_grant_for_bash_command calls _match_loaded_grant directly at line 213 (not _load_and_match), read_grant and read_grant_for_git_command convert via 'result is not None', read_grant signature unchanged, no os.unlink in read functions.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep output: 'candidates: list,' at allowlist.py:40 and allowlist.py:99; AC1 requires 'candidates: list[str]'

success_criteria_results.criterion: _check_git_allowlist body has no open(flag_path or fcntl.flock; imports read_grant_for_git_command; IS_SUBAGENT check before lib call
success_criteria_results.details: Line 92: 'from lib.allowlist import read_grant_for_git_command'. Lines 144-155: _check_git_allowlist body has no open/flock; IS_SUBAGENT check (data.get('agent_id')) at line 150 before lib call at line 155. No independent grant-file open.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct file read of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py lines 92 and 144-155

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Force-push ordering preserved in git guard
success_criteria_results.details: _push_has_forbidden_ref_mutation at line 554, _check_git_allowlist at line 561. Ordering invariant preserved. Force-push block returns before reaching allowlist check.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct file read of pretool-git-privilege-guard.py confirming line 554 < line 561

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: consume_grant_for_posttool is the sole tool-execution consumption unlink site
success_criteria_results.criterion: posttool-allowlist-consume.py has no os.unlink adjacent to claude-bash-allowlist; lib/allowlist.py has exactly one os.unlink (inside consume_grant_for_posttool)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: grep os.unlink in posttool and allowlist.py
success_criteria_results.details: posttool-allowlist-consume.py (51 lines): grep os.unlink returns NOT FOUND. lib/allowlist.py: exactly one os.unlink at line 296 inside consume_grant_for_posttool. stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh turn-end expiry rm -f is explicitly out of scope per AC4 note.
success_criteria_results.evidence: File read and grep verification of posttool-allowlist-consume.py and lib/allowlist.py

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: consume_grant_for_posttool signature, atomicity, and branch policy correct
success_criteria_results.criterion: signature (sid, tool_name, command) -> bool; fcntl.LOCK_EX before match; os.unlink while lock held; non-Bash literal uses exact-only; Bash uses compound-split + full command + tool_name fallback
success_criteria_results.details: Signature confirmed at line 229. fcntl.flock(fh, LOCK_EX) at line 256. os.unlink at line 296 inside the 'with open' block (lock still held). Non-Bash literal uses pattern == tool_name (exact-only) at line 292 — prevents /allow Write consuming TodoWrite. Bash branch at line 268 implements compound split + full command + tool_name fallback.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: posttool still builds and imports correctly
success_criteria_results.criterion: posttool imports consume_grant_for_posttool; calls it with (sid, tool_name, command); is_subagent_context check before lib call; independent open/flock/unlink block removed
success_criteria_results.details: Line 23: 'from lib.allowlist import consume_grant_for_posttool'. Line 34: is_subagent_context(data) before lib call. Line 45: consume_grant_for_posttool(session_id, tool_name, command). Line 46: sys.exit(0). No independent open/flock/os.unlink block anywhere in file.
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct read of posttool-allowlist-consume.py (51 lines total)

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Bash heredoc is a shim (no independent grant-file open, no duplicated logic)
success_criteria_results.criterion: heredoc does not contain open(flag_file or json.load/fcntl.flock/claude-bash-allowlist-/flag_path/flag_file/safe_search; imports via sys.path from HOOKS_DIR env; CONSUMED protocol emitted; IS_SUBAGENT in bash wrapper
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 130-229; grep for forbidden identifiers in lines 163-183
success_criteria_results.details: Lines 163-183: 16-line shim contains only sys.path.insert, import match_grant_for_bash_command, call, and CONSUMED protocol print. Confirmed absence of json.load, fcntl.flock, claude-bash-allowlist-, flag_path, flag_file, safe_search in the shim. HOOKS_DIR set via BASH_SOURCE at line 164 before heredoc. IS_SUBAGENT check at line 145 in bash wrapper. CONSUMED token matches case pattern.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: IS_SUBAGENT firewall — subagent cannot consume any grant
success_criteria_results.criterion: git guard returns False for agent_id payload; bash hook returns 1 for IS_SUBAGENT=1; posttool exits before consume; lib/allowlist.py contains NO reference to agent_id, is_subagent_context, or IS_SUBAGENT
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Read lib/allowlist.py; grep IS_SUBAGENT in file; run 25-test suite including IS_SUBAGENT scenario
success_criteria_results.details: Functional IS_SUBAGENT firewalls are intact in all callers. 25/25 unit tests pass including IS_SUBAGENT scenario. However: hooks/lib/allowlist.py lines 9, 152, and 177 contain the literal token 'IS_SUBAGENT' in docstring/comments. AC8 states 'the file contains NO reference to agent_id, is_subagent_context, or IS_SUBAGENT'. Comments are references. This is a literal AC violation despite zero runtime impact.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep output: 'IS_SUBAGENT' found at allowlist.py:9 (module docstring), :152 (function docstring), :177 (function docstring). AC8 requires NO reference.

success_criteria_results.criterion: pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, pretool-subagent-enforce.py, pretool-aggregate-check.py still import read_grant with no signature change
success_criteria_results.verification_method: grep import statements in the three unchanged callers
success_criteria_results.details: All three files import read_grant from lib.allowlist with the original signature. Dev report blast_radius confirms these files were not modified and import read_grant unchanged.
success_criteria_results.evidence: dev_report blast_radius confirms pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, pretool-subagent-enforce.py, pretool-aggregate-check.py imported-by list; grep confirms read_grant import in each

success_criteria_results.criterion: _load_and_match is underscore-prefixed; not directly imported by any hook file
success_criteria_results.verification_method: grep '_load_and_match' and '_match_loaded_grant' in hooks/ excluding lib/allowlist.py and tests/
success_criteria_results.details: Both _match_loaded_grant and _load_and_match are underscore-prefixed (private). No hook file imports either function directly — only the test file imports them for direct unit testing, which is acceptable.
success_criteria_results.evidence: grep confirms no imports outside lib/allowlist.py and hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: pretool-write-guard.sh no longer contains independent grant-file open
success_criteria_results.criterion: file does not contain open( adjacent to _WG_ALLOW_FILE or claude-bash-allowlist; no fcntl.flock or json.load in /allow section; delegates to lib.allowlist.read_grant via subprocess; IS_SUBAGENT and /do checks before lib call; bash -n exits 0
success_criteria_results.details: Lines 32-46: replaced with heredoc subprocess calling read_grant('Write', sid) via HOOKS_DIR. No open( adjacent to _WG_ALLOW_FILE, no fcntl.flock or json.load in /allow section. _WG_IS_SUB check at line 21 and _WG_DO_FLAG check at line 28 precede the lib call. bash -n passes (syntax OK).
success_criteria_results.evidence: Direct read of pretool-write-guard.sh (66 lines total); bash -n exit 0 confirmed by dev self-verification

qa._success_criteria_rule: 11 ACs verified; 2 fail on literal annotation/comment-token grounds; 9 pass

root_cause_verification.rationale: All 4 independent open/flock/JSON-load/match implementations have been replaced with library calls. commit aaf3c44 partial migration is now complete. Single edit point for format/semantics changes is achieved. Library functions correctly encapsulate all matching logic with preserved per-hook semantics.

build_verification.skip_reason: Change affects only hook scripts and a Python library — no deployed service was modified. These are offline pre/posttool hooks, not user-facing web services. Syntax verification performed instead: python3 -m py_compile on all modified .py files, bash -n on all modified .sh files — all pass.

regression_test_results.details: python3 -m py_compile hooks/lib/allowlist.py hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py hooks/pretool-orchestrator-gate.py hooks/pretool-subagent-enforce.py hooks/pretool-aggregate-check.py — all pass
bash -n hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh && bash -n hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh — both exit 0
pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, pretool-subagent-enforce.py, pretool-aggregate-check.py still import read_grant from lib.allowlist with original signature; no import errors

e2e_enforcement.health_check_result: N/A — hook files only, no web service modified

generated_tests.description: 25 unit tests covering _match_loaded_grant, _load_and_match, read_grant, read_grant_for_git_command, match_grant_for_bash_command, consume_grant_for_posttool. Includes AC8 IS_SUBAGENT firewall scenarios: (a) git guard returns False for agent_id payload, (b) non-Bash exact-only prevents Write consuming TodoWrite, (c) Bash compound command consumed correctly.

all_findings.issue: AC1 type annotation mismatch: _match_loaded_grant parameter annotated as 'candidates: list' but AC1 specifies 'candidates: list[str]'. No runtime impact; literal AC signature requirement not met.
all_findings.recommendation: Change annotation to 'candidates: list[str]' at allowlist.py:40 and allowlist.py:99

all_findings.issue: AC8 literal requirement violated: AC8 states 'the file contains NO reference to IS_SUBAGENT'. allowlist.py contains IS_SUBAGENT at lines 9 (module docstring), 152 (function docstring), 177 (function docstring). No runtime impact; comments explain caller responsibility.
all_findings.recommendation: Reword the three IS_SUBAGENT mentions to avoid the exact token, e.g. replace with 'caller firewall stays in each caller; this library has no subagent awareness' and similarly for function docstrings.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 3 findings: (1) AC1 signature mismatch — 'candidates: list' vs 'candidates: list[str]' is technically false per AC1 text. PROPOSED_FIX: change to list[str]. (2) AC8 literal 'NO reference' violated by IS_SUBAGENT in comments at lines 9, 152, 177. PROPOSED_FIX: reword comments. (3) Test overclaims firewall — no monkeypatch proving lib was not called under subagent. PROPOSED_FIX: add monkeypatch tests. Codex classified all as 'PASS-conditioning issues rather than a hard FAIL; no functional bypass-set regression found.'
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1 and 2 incorporated as FAIL findings (literal AC non-compliance per Anti-Fraud Principle 3 — must verify FULL acceptance criteria). Finding 3 classified as out_of_scope observation — implementation line order is provably correct, library has no agent_id awareness, and the test does confirm the return value is False. Monkeypatch hardening is a test improvement, not a current bug. Codex observation_only on finding 3 recorded in out_of_scope_observations.

failed_criteria: AC1: change 'candidates: list' to 'candidates: list[str]' in _match_loaded_grant (line 40) and _load_and_match (line 99) in hooks/lib/allowlist.py
AC8: remove IS_SUBAGENT token from 3 comment locations in hooks/lib/allowlist.py (lines 9, 152, 177) — reword to avoid the exact string, e.g. 'caller firewall stays in each caller'
refined_context.recommended_approach: Two targeted one-line annotation changes and three comment rewordings in hooks/lib/allowlist.py. No logic changes needed. Re-run 25 unit tests to confirm they still pass after the annotation change (list[str] is backward compatible in Python 3.12). Both fixes are trivial.
refined_context.additional_context: All invariants (IS_SUBAGENT firewall, sole os.unlink, force-push ordering, non-Bash exact-only policy, CONSUMED protocol, HOOKS_DIR pattern) are correctly implemented and functionally verified. User requirement is satisfied. Only literal AC text compliance is blocking close.
